name: Basel Honey-Spice Cookies (Basel Leckerli)
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Kirsch (cherry brandy) or orange juice
Finely grated peel (yellow part only) of one lemon
1/4 cup Finely chopped candied lemon peel
1/4 cup Finely chopped candied orange peel
2/3 cup Finely chopped blanched slivered almonds
2 and 1/4 cups All-purpose or unbleached white flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Generously grease two baking sheets, at least 10-by-14 inches each.
3. In a medium saucepan, combine honey, sugar, and kirsch (or orange juice). Heat over medium heat until the sugar is completely dissolved and the mixture is hot but not boiling.
4. Remove from heat and stir in cinnamon, cloves, lemon peel, candied peels, and almonds until fully incorporated.
5. Gradually add flour to the pan while stirring vigorously; the mixture will become stiff. If it feels too soft or sticky, add a bit more flour; avoid overflouring.
6. Set aside until cool enough to handle (barely warm).
7. Sprinkle a work surface with flour and roll out half of the dough into an 8-1/2-by-13-inch rectangle. Repeat with remaining dough.
8. Transfer rolled dough to prepared baking sheets; prick lightly all over with fork tines.
9. Bake in the center third of the oven for 13 to 16 minutes, or until edges are slightly darker and cookies are barely set. Avoid overbaking.
10. Loosen edges from pans using a spatula and transfer to cutting board.
11. Remove any dry edges, then score rectangles lengthwise and crosswise into 1-by-2 1/2-inch bars.
12. Return scored rectangles to baking sheets.
13. In small saucepan, whisk together powdered sugar, vanilla, and 3 tablespoons water until smooth.
14. Boil mixture briefly until glossy and translucent.
15. Divide glaze among rectangles; spread quickly over entire surface.
16. Let stand until glaze is set (about 1 hour).
17. Bend or cut bars along score lines when glaze is dry and hard.
18. Store airtight for up to 3 weeks.
